California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission '-
John Stremple, Superintendent 
Fresno Unified School District 
Tulare and M Streets 
Fresno, CA 
93721 
Dear Mr. Stremple: 
December 16, 1985 
Re: 
Our File No. A-85-237 
This is in response to your letter of November 6, 1985, 
regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political 
Reform Act (Government Code Sections 81000-91015). 
It is my 
understanding that pursuant to a telephone conversation between 
Nancy Smith of the County Counsel's office and Robert Leidigh 
of the Commission's legal staff, your request for a formal 
opinion has been changed to a request for written advice under 
FPPC regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18329. 
Your letter asked whether the Fresno Unified School District 
had made a "contribution," as defined in Government Code Section 
82015, to the Fresno Teachers Association by allowing the union 
to use the district's internal mail system to distribute material 
endorsing three school board candidates. 
Because the union regularly uses the district's internal 
mail system to communicate with its members pursuant to a col-
lective bargaining agreement between the union and the district, 
the union's use of the internal mail system to distribute mail 
endorsing candidates would not constitute a contribution from 
the district to the union. 
This advice would not apply, of course, 
if some other entity, such as a corporation, allowed the union to 
use its internal mail system to distribute such materials. 
I hope the foregoing has been helpful. 
Please let me know 
if I can be of any assistance in the future. 
Sincerely, 
(] ().;\it:-~Ja-~d-t~ 
Carla Wardlow 
Political Reform Consultant 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660 


BOARD OF EDUCATION 
Darlene M, Laval, Presi(jQnt 
Ruth M, Gadebu:sch, Clt)rk

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
428 IIJII Street, Suite 700 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
Gentlemen: 
THE 
William

Management Services 
Gordon Graves 
Directcr 
Employer'Employee Relations 
I am writing to request a formal opinion from your office 
regarding the use by the Fresno Teachers Association of the 
union members' mailboxes maintained by the Fresno Unified 
School District. 
QUESTION PRESENTED 
The specific question is: 
union to use the internal mail 
enclosed material constitute a 
Government Code Section 82015? 
BACKGROUND 
does the district's allowing the 
system to distribute the 
"contribution" for purposes of 
Government Code Section 3543.1 Subdivision (b) provides in 
part that employee organizations shall have the right to use 
institutional mailboxes and other means of communication. 
In 
addition, the collective bargaining agreement currently in 
effect between Fresno Unified School District and the Fresno 
Teachers Association/CTA/NEA provides in Article 3 Section 2 in 
part: 
i 
commun cation to teachers." 
i 
f 
1 
__________________ ~/~I~-----------------
W 


Fair Political Practices Commission 
Page Two 
November 6. 1985 
The attached material was sent through the district mail 
service to union members in October. 1985. 
A number of 
candidates for the school board have complained to the district 
regarding the use of the mailboxes by the union to support 
certain candidates. 
Their allegation apparently is that the 
district has made a contribution within the meaning of 
Government Code Section 82015 to the union and should have the 
obligation to report that contribution to the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
Ms. Nancy Smith. Deputy County Counsel. contacted your 
office on our behalf on October 24. 1985 and spoke to Ms. Carla 
Wardlow. 
Ms. Wardlow informed Ms. Smith. after discussing the 
matter with the head of your legal department. that there was 
no contribution from the district to the union that would 
require reporting since it was the regular policy of the 
district to let the union use the internal mail system and 
since the district did not take any different action regarding 
this union mailing than it had taken with the other 
distributions of the mail by the union to the union members. 
This conclusion would be the same. Ms. Wardlow indicated. 
regardless of whether the political message was contained in 
the regular union newsletter or whether it was a special 
distribution just for the purpose of getting out the union's 
message regarding an election. 
That information was given to the public at the regular 
board meeting of the Fresno Unified School District Board of 
Education on October 24. 1985. 
Apparently not satisfied with 
that information. a Mr. Paul Winter on behalf of Mr. John 
Segura. a candidate for the Board of Education. contacted 
County Counsel. indicating that he had spoken to Ms. Lynn 
Montgomery of your office and that Ms. Montgomery had informed 
him that in fact there was a violation of the Political Reform 
Act. 
Upon checking with Ms. Montgomery. she indicated that she 
had not spoken to Mr. Winter and had given no opinion to the 
effect that there was a violation. 
As you can see then. with the conflicting oral 
have heard it would be most advantageous for us to 
it en op ni 
f om 
offi 
t 
t we 
ould 
ha 
reports we 
have a 
t 
er 
ted 
ndida es and 
th the public at large and 
insure that the district can be certain of the appropri tene s 
of its actions. 


Fair Political Practices Commission 
Page Three 
November 6, 1985 
I would appreciate your earliest response to my request. 
If you require any further information please feel free to 
contact Ms. Nancy Smith directly at the Fresno County Counsel1s 
Office. Thank you for your attention to my request.

California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
November 19, 1985 
John Stremple, Superintendent 
Fresno Unified School District 
Education Center 
Tulare and M Streets 
Fresno, CA 
93721 
Re: 
A-85-237 
Dear Mr. Stremple: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act has been referred to a consultant in the Technical 
Assistance and Analysis Division of the Fair Political 
Practices commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact this person directly at 
(916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or unless more information is needed to answer your request, 
you should expect a response within 21 working days. 
BAM:plh 
Very truly yours, 
Barbara A. Milman 
General Counsel 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916) 322~5660 


CALIFORNIA STATE DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION 
721 Capitol Mall 
Sacramento, CA 95814-4785 
December II, 1985 
Robert D. Segura 
5716 N. Fourth Street 
Fresno, CA 
93710 
Dear Mr. Segura: 
Bill Honig 
Superintendent 
of Public Instruction 
Superintendent Honig has referred your letter to me for reply. 
Education Code 
S~tions 7050-7057 , eoclosed, relate to R>litical Activities of School Officers 
and Einployees. 
While these sections do give to the governing board of your 
district a certain amount of authOLity to establish rules and regulations 
regarding the political activities of employees during working hours, Section 
7054 specifically prohibits the use of school ~district furrls, services, 
supplies, or equiprrent to urge the cand idacy of any person for election to the 
governing board of the district. 
We are not, however, the legal advisers to 
your district. Further questions should be addressed to the district'"s 
counsel. 
'!he governing board of your school district has the authority to resolve this 
matter. 
Your corrplaints regarding the contents of the teachers'" agreement 
itself may be lcdged with the Public Einploynent Relations Board, 1031 18th 
Street, Sacramento, CA 95814. 
JRS:sca 
cc: Fair R>litical Practices Commission 
Edward W. Hunt, Fresro County Distr ict Attorney 
American Civil Liberties Uhion 
~losure

It is my understanding that pursuant to a telephone conversation between Nancy Smith of the County Counsel's office and Robert Leidigh of the Commission's legal staff, your request for a formal opinion has been changed to a request for written advice under FPPC regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18329. Your letter asked whether the Fresno Unified School District had made a "contribution," as defined in Government Code Section 82015, to the Fresno Teachers Association by allowing the union to use the district's internal mail system to distribute material endorsing three school board candidates. Because the union regularly uses the district's internal mail system to communicate with its members pursuant to a col- lective bargaining agreement between the union and the district, the union's use of the internal mail system to distribute mail endorsing candidates would not constitute a contribution from the district to the union. This advice would not apply, of course, if some other entity, such as a corporation, allowed the union to use its internal mail system to distribute such materials. I hope the foregoing has been helpful. Please let me know if I can be of any assistance in the future.
